title: Use Simple Class Names in Java
description: Java's import mechanism allows the use of simple class names. Using a class' fully qualified name in a file that imports the class is redundant and confusing. It's suggested to use simple class names for code clarity and ease of reading.
recommendation: Instead of using a class' fully qualified name, use simple class names after importing the class. For example, use 'List<String> myList;' instead of 'java.util.List<String> myList;'.
impact: Improves code readability and reduces confusion

checks.message: Use simple class names instead of fully qualified names when the class is imported
